title: Nikos Tsapatsoulis
text:
Professor at the Department of Public Communication, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Image Analysis and Synthesis from the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National Technical University of Athens (2000). His research has been recognised by the international research community through more than 4000 independent citations.
Research interests: Multimedia Information Retrieval, Web Data Mining, Recommender Systems, Collective Intelligence, Affective Computing.
Email: nicolas.tsapatsoulis@cut.ac.cy

title: Nelly Sergidou
text:
PhD student at the Cyprus University of Technology under the supervision of Associate Professor Vasiliki Triga. Her research focuses on news media and social media agenda in the context of referendum campaigns. Has been involved in European and Horizon projects investigating how European societies deal with their troubled pasts through the analysis of conflict discourses and their impact on European integration.
Research interests: News Media and Social Media, Referendum Campaigns, Electoral Campaigns.

title: Kostas Gemenis
text:
Assistant Professor of Politics and the Internet in the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology. Ph.D. in Politics and International Relations from Keele University, UK. Published in Political Communication, Communication Methods and Measures, and Electoral Studies. Co-designed a module to study conspiracy theories related to the COVID-19 pandemic for the 10th Round of the European Social Survey (ESS).
Research interests: Voting Advice Applications, Political Communication, Quantitative Text Analysis, Social Media and Conspiracy Beliefs.

title: Antonis Charalampous
text:
PhD student at the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology, under the supervision of Associate Professor Constantinos Djouvas. His research focuses on developing new techniques and systems for analysing online discourse.
Research interests: Online Discourse Analysis, Argumentation, Social Media.
Email: ag.charalampous@cut.ac.cy

title: Research
text: DIGIPOLS research is organised around four clusters that bring together social scientists and computer scientists working on complementary areas.

title: Research
text:
Applied Political Communication
Political communication is concerned with how information is exchanged between political actors, from politicians and governments to civil society and citizens, and the role played by intermediary actors, such as the news media, in this transmission. The communication linkages are multi-faceted and increasingly digitalised. This cluster is focused on ‘applied’ aspects of political communication that involve experimentation, such as lab experiments and field experiments. Most of the ongoing research is associated with the pioneering work of DIGIPOLS researchers on Voting Advice Applications.

title: Research
text:
Media and Social Media Monitoring
This research area is mainly centred on the development of data collection platforms that track particular domains of interest. Work has been conducted on building real-time data collection during election campaigns, for the evaluation of the communication of policy by the EU, as well as monitoring the latter’s linguistic communication practices.

title: Research
text:
Public Opinion & Argument Mining
This research area is concerned with developing techniques and tools for the analysis of public opinion. It includes both traditional forms of public opinion, such as surveys, and more recent big data approaches to opinion mining. A complementary area of research is focused on argument mining, which draws inspiration from computational linguistics.

title: Research
text:
DIGIPOLS Forum
The Forum is an umbrella organisation that periodically brings together DIGIPOLS researchers. The Forum keeps researchers abreast of the state-of-the-art and serves as a venue for the exchange of ideas. Crucially, the Forum provides strategic input and guidance to DIGIPOLS more specialised research clusters. It is also responsible for organising DIGIPOLS events.

title: DigiPols
text: DIGIPOLS focuses on the intersection of digital technologies and politics. Our mission is to provide a research environment for pursuing cutting-edge, theory-driven empirical research on how digital technologies are impacting our institutions of governance and how politics shapes the development of these technologies.

title: About
text:
DIGIPOLS focuses on the intersection of digital technologies and politics. Its mission is to provide a research environment for pursuing cutting-edge, theory-driven empirical research for advancing the study of how digital technologies are impacting our institutions of governance and how politics shapes the development of these technologies.
As an inter-departmental entity, DIGIPOLS pursues an interdisciplinary approach that brings together social scientists and computer scientists conducting complementary research in areas related to DIGIPOLS thematic interests. The lab’s researchers are brought together in the DIGIPOLS Forum, which meets regularly to exchange ideas, discuss best practices and offer strategic input to the four DIGIPOLS research clusters.

title: About
text:
Research Areas
DIGIPOLS research is organised around four clusters:
Applied Political Communication — Research on how political information is exchanged between political actors and citizens, with a focus on experimental approaches and Voting Advice Applications.
Media and Social Media Monitoring — Development of data collection platforms that track political communication across news media and social media, including real-time monitoring during election campaigns.
Public Opinion & Argument Mining — Development of techniques and tools for the analysis of public opinion, combining traditional survey approaches with computational methods and argument mining.
DIGIPOLS Forum — An umbrella organisation that periodically brings together DIGIPOLS researchers to exchange ideas and provide strategic input to the research clusters.

title: Team
text:

title: Constantinos Djouvas
text:
Associate Professor at the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Computer Networks from the City University of New York. Has participated in numerous EU-funded research projects and published in various journals related to Computer Science and Social and Political Science.
Research interests: Big Data, Argument Mining, Social Media Analysis.
Email: costas.tziouvas@cut.ac.cy

title: Nikandros Ioannides
text:
PhD student at Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona under the supervision of Professor Toni Rodon. Holds a master’s degree in Democracy and Comparative Politics from University College London (UCL) and a bachelor’s degree in Communication and Internet Studies from the Cyprus University of Technology (CUT).
Research interests: Contemporary Democracies, Party Politics, Political Participation, Voter-Party Congruence, Public Opinion.

title: Vasiliki Triga
text:
Associate Professor at the Department of Public Communication, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Social and Political Sciences from the European University Institute, Florence. Published in IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics, Southern European Society and Politics, Journal of Common Market Studies, and Public Management. Co-author of Referendums and the European Union: A Comparative Inquiry (Cambridge University Press, 2014).
Research interests: Political Communication, Media and Social Media, Political Parties, Electoral Campaigns.
Email: vasiliki.triga@cut.ac.cy
